title: Java static Keyword
content:
### The `static` Keyword

The `static` keyword means the member belongs to the class itself, not to instances.

---

### Static Fields

Shared across all instances of a class:

```java
public class Counter {
    static int count = 0;  // shared by all instances
    Counter() {
        count++;
    }
}
```

---

### Static Methods

Methods that can be called without creating an object:

```java
Math.max(10, 20);  // static method call
Integer.parseInt("123");  // static method call
```

Rules for static methods:
- Can only access static fields/methods directly
- Cannot use `this` keyword
- Called using class name: `ClassName.method()`

---

### Static Block

Executed once when the class is first loaded:

```java
class Database {
    static {
        System.out.println("Loading database driver...");
    }
}
```

---

### Static vs Instance

| Feature | Static | Instance |
|---------|--------|----------|
| Memory | One copy (class area) | Per object (heap) |
| Access | `ClassName.member` | `object.member` |
| `this` usage | Not allowed | Allowed |
| Override | No (hidden, not overridden) | Yes |

> **Key Point:** Static members are loaded when the class is first loaded into memory. The `main` method is static because it must be callable without creating any object first.

quiz.question: Do static methods belong to the class or instance?
Can a static method access instance fields directly?
